prompt: The pixel coordinates throw ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException. What is the bug?
choices: setRGB requires ARGB packed values; 0xFF0000 is not a valid color
BufferedImage pixel coordinates use 1-based indexing starting at (1,1)
x=10 is out of bounds for an image of width 10; valid x-coordinates are 0 through 9 (inclusive)
setRGB cannot be called on TYPE_INT_RGB images; use TYPE_INT_ARGB
explanation: BufferedImage uses 0-based pixel coordinates. For width=10, valid x values are 0–9. Passing x=10 is out of bounds and throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException. Fix: use img.setRGB(9, 5, 0xFF0000) or validate x < img.getWidth() before calling setRGB.
